message:
fix(sync,net,scheduler): rwlock write fairness, UDP nonblocking, scheduler tuning

Three independent correctness and performance fixes across the runtime:

sync/rwlock.rs — Fix try_write() bypassing queued async writers:
- try_write() previously only checked writer_active and readers, allowing
  it to barge ahead of a queued async write() that was waiting for a read
  lock to release. Now also checks writer_waiters > 0, preserving FIFO
  queue ordering and preventing writer starvation.
- Add test: try_write_does_not_bypass_waiting_writer_turn validates that
  after a read guard is dropped, a queued writer gets priority over a
  subsequent try_write() call.

net/udp.rs — Force nonblocking mode in UdpSocket::from(StdUdpSocket):
- Callers passing a freshly-created std::net::UdpSocket (which defaults
  to blocking mode) would cause async poll paths to block. Now explicitly
  calls set_nonblocking(true) during conversion.
- Add test: udp_from_std_forces_nonblocking_mode uses fcntl F_GETFL to
  verify O_NONBLOCK is set after conversion (unix-only).

runtime/scheduler/priority.rs — Reduce MIN_DENSE_LEN from 1024 to 256:
- Lowers the threshold for the dense bitvector scheduling optimization,
  allowing smaller task sets to benefit from O(1) membership checks
  without the 8 KiB minimum allocation overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
